skill.name: ab-test-setup
skill.description: Structured guide for setting up A/B tests with mandatory gates for hypothesis, metrics, and execution readiness.
skill.summary: Structured guide for setting up A/B tests with mandatory gates for hypothesis, metrics, and executio...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm and reconnaissance patterns at SKILL.md lines 3, 14, 28, 40, 70, 76, 91, 197, 208, and 232. Each occurrence is ordinary A/B testing prose in a Markdown guide, not executable code or instructions to inspect systems or networks. No evidence found of prompt injection, data exfiltration, command execution, filesystem access, network access, or malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Poorly planned experiments waste traffic and create misleading results. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through hypothesis locks, metric choices, sample size checks, and launch gates.
actual_capabilities: Guides users to define a final hypothesis, audience, primary metric, expected direction, and MDE.
Checks assumptions about traffic stability, user independence, metric reliability, randomization, and external factors.
Helps select A/B, A/B/n, multivariate, or split URL test designs based on traffic and change scope.
Separates primary, secondary, and guardrail metrics before launch.
Enforces sample size, duration, tracking, and execution readiness gates.
Structures test records, result interpretation, decisions, learnings, and follow-up ideas.
limitations: It does not calculate statistical power without user-supplied rates, traffic, and MDE.
It does not connect to analytics platforms or retrieve experiment data.
It does not implement experiment code, tracking code, or randomization logic.
It depends on accurate user inputs and verified measurement systems.

use_cases.title: Define a Product Experiment
use_cases.description: Turn a product idea into a locked hypothesis, test audience, metrics, and readiness checklist.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Conversion Test
use_cases.description: Validate that a landing page or funnel change has clear success criteria and guardrails before launch.

use_cases.title: Review Experiment Validity
use_cases.description: Check assumptions, sample size, duration, and interpretation limits before sharing experiment results.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Test Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a simple two-variant test.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up an A/B test for [change]. Ask for any missing hypothesis, audience, metric, baseline, MDE, and traffic details before creating the plan.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Metrics and Guardrails
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review before an experiment brief is finalized.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this experiment idea and identify the primary metric, secondary metrics, guardrail metrics, and any missing measurement assumptions: [experiment details].

prompt_templates.title: Check Launch Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced pre-launch gate for teams with tracking and traffic estimates.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the execution readiness gate to decide whether this test can launch. Include blockers, required fixes, and a clear go or no-go recommendation: [test plan].

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Completed Experiment
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced result interpretation with guardrails and learning capture.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these A/B test results using the skill principles. Separate statistical significance, business judgment, guardrail outcomes, decision, learning, and follow-up ideas: [results].

output_examples.input: A product manager wants to test a shorter signup form.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a hypothesis lock, target audience, primary metric, MDE requirement, guardrail metrics, sample size inputs, and readiness questions.

output_examples.input: A growth team asks whether it can stop a test after two days of positive results.
output_examples.output: The skill advises against early stopping, checks the planned duration and sample size, and recommends monitoring technical health instead.

output_examples.input: An analyst has an inconclusive result with no guardrail issues.
output_examples.output: The skill frames the outcome as inconclusive, limits claims to the tested population, and suggests more traffic or a bolder change.

best_practices: Lock the hypothesis, primary metric, audience, MDE, and duration before launch.
Use guardrail metrics to prevent harmful wins from shipping.
Document assumptions, external factors, achieved sample size, decision, and learning.
anti_patterns: Do not stop early because interim results look favorable.
Do not change variants, traffic sources, or success criteria during the test.
Do not ship a primary metric win when guardrail metrics fail.

faq.question: Does this skill run A/B tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides planning, validation, readiness, and interpretation. It does not deploy experiments or collect data.

faq.question: Can it choose the right test type?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides selection among A/B, A/B/n, multivariate, and split URL tests based on the proposed change and traffic.

faq.question: Does it calculate sample size?
faq.answer: It requires sample size planning and can structure the inputs. Users must provide reliable baseline rates, traffic, and MDE.

faq.question: How does it prevent invalid conclusions?
faq.answer: It enforces a locked hypothesis, frozen primary metric, guardrails, duration discipline, and limits on result interpretation.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-technical teams?
faq.answer: Yes. Product, growth, and analytics teams can use it to create experiment briefs before engineering work begins.

faq.question: What data should I prepare first?
faq.answer: Prepare the user problem, target audience, baseline rate, traffic volume, proposed change, success metric, and known external factors.
